English: What optimal ecological requirements for cabbage?
Kikuyu: Hihi nīmaūndū marīkū maria matūrigicīire magīrīire ūkūria-inī wa kabicī?

English: 700 – 2,200 metres above sea level altitude.At least 500 mm of rainfall.16 – 20 0C growing temperature.
Kikuyu: mita 700 nginya mita 2,200 igūrū wa iria. Mbura ītanyihīire 500 mm na ūrugarī wa ithimo cia 16 degree Celsius

English: Well drained sandy or silty loam soils.High organic matter content.pH range 6.0 – 6.5
Kikuyu: Tīri ūraikūrūkia maaī wega kana tīri mūirū mūhinyu. Tīri mūnoru mūingī na ūrūrū wa gītagatī kuuma 6.0 pH nginya 6.5 pH.

English: What are the growing requirements for cabbages in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū mabataranagia ūkūria-inī wa mboga būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: Cabbage is a cool season crop which requires an optimum growth temperatures range of 15- 20° C.
Kikuyu: Mboga nī mūmera wa kūndū kūrī heho na ūbataraga ūrugarī wa ithimo cia kuuma 15°C nginya 20°C

English: Head formation reduced at temperatures higher than 25 degrees Celsius.
Kikuyu: Gūkūmba kwa mboga  nīkūnyihaga ūrugarī ūngīthiī makīria ma 25°C

English: It requires adequate well distributed rainfall.
Kikuyu:
Ībataranagia mbura ya kūigana
 kīmera gīothe.

English: Moisture levels are especially critical during the early stage of the crop growth.
Kikuyu: ūigū wa tīri nīwa bata makīria kīambīrīria-inī kīa gūkūra kwa mūmera

English: If the levels are low, irrigation should be used to supplement and relive the moisture stress.
Kikuyu: Angīkorūo tīri ndūrī na wūigū wa kūigana, hebata gūitīīrīria maaī nīgūo kūehereria mūtangīko owothe ūngīrehwo nī wagi wa maaī.

English: What is the recommended fertilizer rate for cabbage growing?
Kikuyu: Hihi nī mūigana ūrīkū wa bataraitha wagīrīire kūhūthīrwo mūgūnda-inī wa mboga?

English: Apply 2 – 3 handfuls of manure per planting hole (8 tons/īka) and 2 half-litre water bottle tops (10 g) of DAP per planting hole (80 kg/īka).DAP should not be applied on acid soils, use DSP, TSP or NPK .
Kikuyu: Īkīra ngundi igirī nginya ithatū cīa thumu o irima (ūū nīkuga ūkūhūthīra tani inyanya cia thumu kūhanda īka ya mboga) ūcoke wongerere ngūnīko īmwe ya cuba ya maaī(gramu ikūmi) ya bataraitha mūthemba wa DAP irima-inī rīu (ūū nīkuga kiro mīrongo īnana cia bataraitha nīikūhūthīka kūhanda mboga īka īmwe). DAP ndīagīrīirwo kūhūthīrwo mūgūnda-inī ūrīna tīri ūrūrītio nī acid, hūthīra bataraitha mīthemba ya DSP, TSP kana NPK.

English: Only thoroughly decomposed manure should be used to avoid possible introduction of cutworms in the field.
Kikuyu: O thumu ūrīa mūhīu wega noguo ūbatiī kūhūthīka nīguo kūgirīrīria kūrehe igunyū mūgūnda-ini.

English: The DAP fertilizer should be mixed thoroughly with the soil to avoid possible scorching of the seedlings.
Kikuyu: Bataraitha ya DAP yagīrīirwo gūtukanio wega na tīri nīguo ndīgacine mīūngūrwa

English: When is top dressing done and  which fertilizer should be used in top dressing of cabbage?
Kikuyu: Bataraitha ya mathangū īkagīrwo ihinda rīrīkū na nī īrīku njega gūīkīra Kabici-ini

English: The crop should be top dressed with CAN fertilizer in 2 splits to avoid nutrient loss through leaching as well as excessive soil salinity.
Kikuyu: Bataraitha ya mathangū ni CAN na yagīrīirwo gūīkīrwo maita merī nī ūndū wa kūmenyerera gūte īrio cia bata harī mūmera na cīngīura kūhetūkīra maaī maingī kana uongerere ūrūrū tīri-ini.

English: The first split is applied at a rate of 10 g/plant (100 kg/īka) 2 – 3 weeks after transplanting.
Kikuyu: Rita rīa mbere yagīrīirwo nīguīkirwo gramu ikūmi harīo mūmera (100kg/īka) wiki 2-3 thutha wa kūhanda

English: The second split is applied at a rate of 20 gm/plant (200 kg/īka) at the onset of head formation.
Kikuyu: Rita rīa kerī yagīrīirwo nīgūīkīrwo gramu 20 harīo mūmera (200kg/īka) rīrīa mboga īrakūnja.

English: Placement method is preferred over broadcasting as it is more effective and economical
Kikuyu: Gūīkīra bataraitha gītinaini nīyo njīra īrīa njega tondū  nīnyihagia hathara

English: What amount of manure should I use to plant cabbage?
Kikuyu: Njagīrīrwo kūhūthira thumu ūigana atīa kūhanda kabici?

English: At planting, apply 2 – 3 handfuls of manure per planting hole (8 tons/īka) and (10 g) of DAP per planting hole (80 kg/īka).
Kikuyu: ūkīhanda īkīra ngundi 2-3 cīa thumu o harī irima rīa kūhanda (tani 8 harī īka) na giramu ikūmi cia DAP o harī irima rīa kūhanda. (kilo 80 hari īka)

English: What are the deficiencies of potassium in cabbage?
Kikuyu: Hihi ūnyihu wa Potasium wīyonanagia atīa harī Kabicī

English: Potassium (K) deficiency can result in marginal necrosis and lower head quality, but its excess can cause the heads to open (NB: Fertilizer application rates is guided by soil and leaf analysis
Kikuyu: Potassium īngīnyīha harī mūmera nīigiragīrīria mboga gūkūmba wega. ūingi wayo nītumaga kabici yatūke

English: Which material should I use in cabbage mulching?
Kikuyu: Nī indo irīku cingītūmīka kwarīra kabici mūceni?

English: Mulching of the soil with material such as crop residues, compost or plastic sheeting to reduce water loss, splashing and break some pest life cycles
Kikuyu: Gūtandīkīra no ūhūthire mahuti ma mīmera, thumu kana minyore nīguo kūgirīrīria kūra kwa maaī, na kūraga tūtambī

English: What nutrients are important in cabbage production?
Kikuyu: Nikī mboga ībataraga nīgetha ikūre wega?

English: Cabbage needs plenty of nutrients. NPK is needed for head formation.
Kikuyu: Mboga ībataraga bataraitha ya NPK nigetha īkūmbe wega.

English: However, excess nitrogen (N) may cause loose head formation and internal decay.
Kikuyu: No nitrogen nyingī no īgirie mboga īkumbe wega na kūbutha thīinī.

English: Potassium (K) deficiency can result in marginal necrosis and lower head quality, but its excess can cause the heads to open.
Kikuyu: Potassium īngīnyīha harī mūmera nīgiragīrīria mboga gūkūmba wega na īngīingīha no yatūke

English: The plants have high Sulfur requirement; sensitive to deficiencies of Calcium, Magnesium, and Boron.
Kikuyu: Mūmera ūngīgīa sulphur nyingī ni unyihagia magnesium na boron.

English: What are the water requirements for cabbage production?
Kikuyu: Mboga ībataraga maaī maigana atīa nīguo īkūre?

English: The optimal amount of rainfall required for Cabbage during the growing period is 500 mm.
Kikuyu: maaī marīa ībataraga ni mirimita 500.

English: Regular watering ensures uniform.Watering should be reduced as crop matures head formation, prevents head splitting and increases the size of the head.
Kikuyu: Gūitīīrīria maaī ūrīa kwagīrīire gūtūmaga kabici ikūre cigananīirie. Wagīrirwo nikūnyihia maaī oūrīa mboga īraneneha kugirīrīria gwatūka na nigetha kabici īnenehe.

English: Excessive watering increases water logging hence deficiencies of Magnesium and Phosphorus.
Kikuyu: ūgunyu mūingī nī utūmaga tīri ūnyihie Magnesium na phosphorus.

English: Use potable water for irrigation for food hygiene.Irrigation can be overhead, drip or furrow
Kikuyu: Hūthīra njīra cia kūhe maaī ūgīthamagia nīguo ūhote kwīiga ūtheru. No ūhūthīre jeti, mīberethi ya kūhe maaī kana mītaro.

English: How do I control weeds in cabbage production?
Kikuyu: Ingīgirīrīria ria atia ūrīmi-inī kabici?

English: Weed control is especially important early in the season when weed competition can substantially reduce vigor, uniformity, and overall yield.
Kikuyu: Kugirīrīria ria rīria rīrī inini nio njīra īrīa njega rītanathūkia na kūnina hinya wa mīmera.

English: Weed control is especially important early in the season when weed competition can substantially reduce vigor, uniformity, and overall yield.
Kikuyu: Kūrīmīra nīgwabata makīria kīambīrīria-inī kīa kīmera rīrīa ria rīhotomagia mūkūrīre wa kabici na thutha-inī maciaro moothe.

English: Optimal production of these Brassica leafy vegetables depends on successful weed control.
Kikuyu: Nīguo ūgīe na magetha mega ma kabici wagīrīirwo kūeheragia ria wega.

English: Weeds reduce yields by direct competition for nutrients, water, and light.
Kikuyu: Ria rīnyihagia magetha nīūndū wa gūtharana na mīmera irio, maaī na ūtheri.

English: Should I remove all crop residues of the previous crop from my filed before planting cabbage?
Kikuyu: Ninjagīrīirwo kūeheria mahuti mothe ma mīmera īria īrahandītwo mbere ya kūhanda mboga?

English: All crop residues should be removed and destroyed by burning or burying in deep pits.
Kikuyu: Mahuti mothe ma mīmera ya hau kabere yagīrīirwo nīgūcinwo kana gūthikuo irima-inī rīriku.

English: This helps prevent last season’s pests including caterpillars, aphids and beetles re-infesting your new crop and also control fungal and bacterial diseases.
Kikuyu: Njīra īno īteithagīrīria kūnina tūtambi tūrīa tūrarī hau mbere, tūgunyu na mīrimu yothe īngītambio harī mīmera mīerū

English: How do I prevent weeds on my cabbage?
Kikuyu: Tūngīgirīrīria ria atīa mūgunda-inī wa mboga?

English: Thorough land preparation, example: by plowing and harrowing
Kikuyu: Kūharīria mīgūnda wega ta gūcimba na kūhūra hianyū

English: • During the seed bed preparation, make sure that the seed bed is free of weeds.
Kikuyu: ū kiharīria tuta wagīrīirwo nigūtigīrīra ndīrī na ria ona hanini

English: • Place the fertilizer where the crop has the access to it but the weeds do not.
Kikuyu: īkīra bataraitha hakuhī na mūmera harīa hatarī na ria.

English: This allows the crop to be more competitive with weeds.
Kikuyu: Īno njīra īteithagia mūmera gūtharana na ria wega

English: • Maintain cleanliness on the drainage canals
Kikuyu: Tigīrīra mītaro ya gūte maaī nī mītheru

English: • Keep the surroundings of your farm free of weeds, unless they are maintained and intended as habitat for natural enemies
Kikuyu: Iga mūgūnda waku ūtarī na ihinga cia ria ūtangīkorwo ndūkwenda gūtheria na ūrenda gūthondeka imamo cia thū

English: • Mulching: Use rice straw as mulch to partially control weed.
Kikuyu: Kwarīra: hūthīra mahuti ma mucere ta kwarīra nīguo kūgiria ria kūmera.

English: Apply straw perpendicularly to the rows at a rate of 5 t/ha.
Kikuyu: Banga mahuti ma mūcere gatagatī ka mīmera mūigana wa tani ithano harī hectare īmwe

English: Apply mulch within a few days after transplanting.
Kikuyu: Arīra thikū nini thutha wa kūhanda

English: • Crop rotation. Do not continuously plant crucifers in the same field.
Kikuyu: ūgacenjania mīmera yaku ndūgacokie mūmera ūrī barī īmwe na ūcio uma ho

English: • Regularly monitor the status of your crops
Kikuyu: Ikara ūkiroraga mīmera yaku ihinda kwa ihinda

English: What are the mechanical.physical ways of controlling weeds on my cabbages?
Kikuyu: Ni njira īrīkū cia macini kana cia moko cia kugirīrīria ria kabici- ini ?

English: Hand weeding. The weeds are easier to control in their earlier growing period.
Kikuyu: Kurīmira na moko- njīra īno īgiragia ria gūkūra rīrī inini.

English: Do not let the weeds to flower.
Kikuyu: Ndukareke ria rīkūre nginya rīīkīre kīro

English: Remove them from the field before they start to flower.
Kikuyu: Rīeherie rītanagīa kīro

English: Weeds bearing seeds should not be placed in compost since seeds may not be killed in the process of decomposition.
Kikuyu: Ria rīrī na mbegū rītikaigirīrwo handū harī na thumu tondū mbegū ndīkuaga handū thumu ūrabutha.

English: Otherwise, compost might be a source of reintroduction of weeds into your fields.
Kikuyu: Ona kūrī ūguo rī, thumu no ūkorwo kīhumo kīa ria.

English: • Hoeing, mowing, and cutting
Kikuyu: Gucimbūrania, kūrima na kwenyūranga ibūru.

English: How do I know that my cabbages are ready for harvest?
Kikuyu: Ingīmenya atīa kabicī nī nginyu kūgethwo?

English: Maturity Period: 2.5 – 4 months after transplanting depending on variety and location
Kikuyu: Mboga īkūraga na mīeri 2-5 kuuma gūthamia tuta-inī kūringana na mūthemba kana kūrīa īkūrīirio.

English: • Maturity: When the head becomes firm
Kikuyu: Gūkūra : kīongo kiomīrīria

English: • Harvest the heads before they pass the prime stage to avoid cracking or splitting
Kikuyu: Getha kabici itanakīria kahinda gagukura nīguo ndīgatūke.

English: • Cut heads at the base and leave the outer leaves to protect the head and keeping it fresh
Kikuyu: Getha mboga itanakīria kahinda gagukura nīguo ndīgathūke.

English: • Avoid bruising the head as it encourages rotting
Kikuyu: Menyerera kūguraria mūtwe wa mboga rīrīa ūrakūmba nīguo ndūkabuthe.

English: What is the average yield of cabbages per īka?
Kikuyu: Īka īkūraga kabici cigana atia?

English: Yields: 15 – 68 tons per īka (depending on the variety and crop husbandry)
Kikuyu: īrutaga tani 15-68 kūringana na mūthemba wa kabici na ūrīa ītungatītuo.

English: How to prepare land for planting?
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda wa kūhanda mboga ūharagīrīrio atīa?

English: Cabbage is a shallow rooted plant; therefore, deep tillage is not required.
Kikuyu: Kabici ndirī mīri mīriku, kwoguo, hatiri bata wa kūrīma ūrikītie mūno.

English: How do I prepare land for transplanting of cabbages seedlings?
Kikuyu: Ūharagīria atīa harīa urathamīria mīungūrwa?

English: Plough and harrow the field until the soil is fine, level and free of weeds and plant debris.
Kikuyu: Cimba na ūhure hianyū wūigananie na weherie ria na mahuti mothe

English: Form the bed with a plow by opening furrows to a depth of 20 cm during the dry season or at least 30 cm during the wet season
Kikuyu: Thondeka tuta na theci na ūkare mīkaro ya 20cm hīndi ya riūa na 30cm hīndi ya mbura

English: What are the steps for nursery establishment?
Kikuyu: Nī makinya marīkū wagīrīirwo kūrūmīrīra ūgīthondeka tuta?

English: Prepare a seedbed of 1 m width and of a convenient length.
Kikuyu: Thondeka tuta ya uraihu wa mita īmwe na wariī kuringana na mūigana wa mbegū ciaku

English: Make drills on the seedbed at a spacing of 10 – 20 cm apart.
Kikuyu: Thondeka tūrima tūnini utigithūkanītie na sentimita 10-20.

English: Thinly sow the seeds in the drills and cover lightly with soil.
Kikuyu: Rekia mbeū tūrima-īni ucoke uthike na gatīri kanini

English: Use certified seed with special attributes, such as tolerance/resistance to pest and diseases and high yielding.
Kikuyu: Handa mbeū irīa hetūkie, irī  na ümīrīru gwītiria tutambi na mīrimū na iciaraga wega.

English: The seed rate is about 100-120 g/īka (depending on variety (F1/OP) and spacing).
Kikuyu: Īka īmwe ībatiī kūhandwo ngiramu 100-120 kūringana na muthemba(F1/OP) na ūrīa watigithūkania

English: What are the management practices in cabbage nurseries?
Kikuyu: Ūmenyagīrīra mboga atīa irī tuta-inī?

English: Water thoroughly after transplanting and regularly.
Kikuyu: Itīīrīria maaī maiganu thutha-inī wa gūthamia kuuma tuta-inī.

English: However, avoid over-watering which can lead to “Damping-off” disease.
Kikuyu: Ona kūrī ūguo rī menyerera gūīkīra maaī maingī ndūkabuthie mīmera

English: Start hardening the seedlings 1 – 2 weeks before transplanting by reducing the frequency of watering.
Kikuyu: Anjīrīria kūnyihia maaī tuta-inī ūtigarītie wiki 1-2 gūthamia nīguo īmenyerere gūcenjia

English: Mulching is important to provide favourable environment for seedlings and the shade over the nursery
Kikuyu: Kwarīra mīmera mūceni nī kwega tondū nīitethagia mīūngūrwa gūkorwo na rīera rīega rīa gūkūra na kīruru natharīīni

English: Why are raised beds important in nursery management?
Kikuyu: Tuta ya kūmbwo īgūrū nīya bata atia harī kūmenyerera mīmera?

English: Raised beds recommended for root development and proper drainages
Kikuyu: Tuta ya kūmbwo īgūrū nīnjega harī mīrita ya mīūngūrwa na gūte maaī

English: • Bed width of 1 meter and a convenient length not exceeding 100 meters and a height of 15 centimeter
Kikuyu: Tuta ya warie wa mita imwe na ūraihu ūtakīrītie mita igana rīmwe na ūriku wa sentimita ikūmi na ithano

English: How is a cabbage nursery bed prepared?
Kikuyu: Tuta ya kūhura kabici īharagīrio atīa?

English: Seedbed is prepared approximately 5 - 15 cm high and 1.0 m wide.
Kikuyu: Tuta yagīrīirwo nīgukorwo na ūriku wa 5-15 cm na warie wa mita īmwe

English: Apply a thin layer of compost on the seedbed before mulching (with rice straw).
Kikuyu: Ongerera thumu mūhīu mūnini īgūrū ūcoke warīre na mahuti ma mūcere

English: In a square meter seedbed, incorporate 40 g ammonium sulfate, 50 g super phosphate, 30 g potassium chloride, and 2 kg/ com post.
Kikuyu: Natharī ya mita imwe o mwena, hūthira gramu 40 cia ammonium sulfate, gramu 50 cia super phosphate, gramu 30 cia potassium Chloride n kilo igirī cia thumu wa mahuti.

English: Commercial inorganic fertilizer could be skipped for organic farming.
Kikuyu: Ndūkahūthīre bataraitha cia matukainī ūkīrīma na njīra ya kīmerera

English: Ask assistance from the local agriculturist office for advice in growing organic cabbage and in order to maintain and supply the nutrient requirement of the plant.
Kikuyu: ūria ūteithio kūri abitha wa ūrīmi, ūkīrīma na njīra ya kīmerera nīguo ndūkanyihie indo cia bata iria kabici īrabatara

English: Protect the seedbed with a net or shed to prevent the seedlings from early pest infestation, heavy rain, and direct sunlight.
Kikuyu: īkira neti ya kīruru tuta-inī nīguo kūgitīra mīūngūrwa kūrīo nī tūtambi, gūkuo nīkīguū kana gūcinwo nīriūa

English: What is the main nutrition importance of cabbage?
Kikuyu: Mboga yongagīrīra kī mīrīini itū?

English: Contains Calcium, Iron, Vitamin A, C & E, Minerals, Riboflavin, Nicotinamine and Ascorbic Acid.
Kikuyu: Kabici ikoraguo na Calcium, Iron, Vitamin A, C & E, Minerals, Riboflavin, Nicotinamine na Ascorbic Acid.

English: Cabbage has cleansing effect of stomach and intestinal tract if consumed raw without salt due to high sulphur and chlorine content
Kikuyu: mboga nīteithagia gūthambia nda na mara īngīrīo īrīnjīthī na ītarī cumbī tondū nīirī na sulphur na chlorine

English: How can cabbage be eaten/used?
Kikuyu: Kabici īngīrīo kana kūhūthīka atīa?

English: Cabbage is used as vegetables can be eaten raw, cooked, boiled, stuffed and sauerkraut.
Kikuyu: Kabici no īrīo ītarī nduge, īrugītwo, īcamūkītio kana ītukanītio thīinī wa indo ingī.

English: The field byproducts are good source of animal feeds.
Kikuyu: Mahuti macio nī irio njega cia ng'ombe

English: What are the advantages of consuming cabbages?
Kikuyu: Nī maūguni marīkū maumanaga na kūrīa mboga?

English: Cabbage contains mustard oil that give them distinctive odor and taste.
Kikuyu: Kabici ikoragwo na maguta ma mūtararīko marīa mamiheyaga mūtararīko wīkūraine na mūcamo.

English: It is an excellent source of minerals such as potassium and high in vitamins A and C. and calcium.
Kikuyu: Ni kihumo kiega kia cumbī ta potassium na vitamin A na C ona calcium

English: Crucifers (cabbage, broccoli, cauliflower, etc) are particularly effective against colon cancer and also protect against cancers of the skin, breast, stomach, bladder, liver and lung;
Kikuyu: Bamīrī ya mboga (kabici, broccoli, cauliflower na ingī) nīiteithagia kūgīrīrīria mūrimū wa cancer ya mara ona ya ngothi, nyondo, nda, bladder, ini na mahūri

English: provided there are not much pesticide residues.
Kikuyu: Angikorwo gūtirī na matigari ma dawa cia kūraga tūtambi

English: What are the major pests that affect cabbage in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī tūtambi tūrīkū tūgūmagīra mboga mūno Kenya?

English: Diamondback Moth (DBM),Cabbage Sawfly,Aphids Slugs Cutworms
Kikuyu: Diamondback Moth (DBM), Sawfly cia mboga, ūme, Ndinoho na tūtengūri

English: How to identify diamond back ?
Kikuyu: ūngīmenya atīa atī kīhuruta nī mūthemba wa Diamond back?

English: Male adult is small grayish moth with diamond pattern on its back when wings are closed.
Kikuyu: Kīhuruta kīa njamba gīkīgima gīkoragwo kīa rangi wa kīmūhū wīcorete na mūhianīre wa diamond rīrīa kīhingīte mathagu.

English: Eggs laid on upper surface of leaves (one female can lay 400 eggs).
Kikuyu: Matumbī marekagio igūrū rīa mathangū (Kīhuruta kīmwe kīa nga no kīrekie matumbī magana mana)

English: What are the damages of diamondback moth in cabbages?
Kikuyu: Nīūūru ūrĩkū ūrehagwo nī kĩhuruta kĩa diamond back thĩinī wa Kabicī?

English: The pale green larvae feed on the underside of leaves making “windows”.
Kikuyu: Kīgunyū kīa diamond back kīrīa mwena wa thīī wa mathangu gīgathodeka icigo ihana tūtirica.

English: Pupation takes place into a silken gauze-like cocoon at underside of leaf.Infestations are normally serious in drier months.
Kikuyu: Namba ino īingīhaga haria hanyoroku thī wa mahuti na nī nyingī makīria icīgoini cia ūrugarī

English: Failure to form heads if infested early
Kikuyu: Mboga nīyagaga gūkūmba īngīgūmīrwo.

English: How is diamondback moth controlled in cabbage?
Kikuyu: Kīhuruta kīa diamond black kīngīgirīrīrio atīa kabici-inī?

English: Use of bio-insecticides such as Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) (DELFIN 6.4W.G® and HALT 50WP®).
Kikuyu: Hūthīra dawa cia tūtambi ta Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) (DELFIN 6.4W.G® and HALT 50WP®).

English: Intercropping with other crops which act as repellants (e.g. Tomato) or trap crops (e.g. Indian Mustard) reduces DBM destruction on Cabbage.
Kikuyu:
Kūhandanīria na mīmera mūthemba ūngī ūrīa ūteithagia kūingata tūtambi (ta nyanya) kana mīmera īrīa īhūthagīrwo ta mūtego (Indian Mustard) nīīnyihagia gūthūkio kwa mboga nī DBM
 .

Kikuyu: Kīonereria kīa Bacteria soft Rot

Kikuyu: Huhīra mūmera na foliar na dawa irīna fungicide ta copper Oxychloride 50%, metallic copper (COBOX 50 WP®) na (ISACOP®) hīndī īria mūmera ūraambirīria kūūngania tūongo

Kikuyu: Hūthīra bactericide ta Bronopol 27% (ENRICH BM)

Kikuyu: Mīri kūongerereka na kūora

Kikuyu: Kūhanda mīmera ngurani

English: • Field hygiene
Kikuyu: Gutheria mūgūnda

English: • Lime application creates soil condition unfavorable for spore formation
Kikuyu: ni gūtūmaha tīri ūkorwo na ikīro irīa ciagīrīire kūrima.

Kikuyu: Kūhanda mbegū cia kabici īria ingīgitia mīrimu na tūtambi

Kikuyu: Gwīka ūū ni kūnyihagia mīeke ya bacteria na fungal īrīa īrehaga mīrimū ta black rot downy gūtherema

Kikuyu: ūū nīkūharīrīria mūgūnda na njīra ya kūhurania tīri gūtigīrīra atī rīūa nīrīrakinyīra tīri wothe kwa ihinda rīa mweri ūmwe

Kikuyu: ūū gūtigīrīra atī mīrimū īrīa yumanaga na tīri, ria na tūtambi twa tīīri -inī niyaga kamweke

Kikuyu: ūrīmi wa mboga ūthingatagio oo kiūmia

Kikuyu: ūndū ūyū nīwa bata mūno makīria wiki cia mbere cia ūkūria wa mboga tondū rīu nīrio ihinda rīrīa mboga īgūmagīrwo nī mīrimu muno

Kikuyu: Tūtambi nī gūtheca tūthecaga mboga nīgetha tūhote kūnyua tūmaaī tūu tokaragwo thīini

Kikuyu: Mahuti marīa mathecangītwo nī tūtambi nī kūbutha maambagīrīria kūbutha hīndī īria mata macio maingīra mahuti -ini.

English: Heavily infested ones will turn yellow and eventually wilt because of excessive sap removal.
Kikuyu: macokaga magacenjia rangi maagatunīha na magekūnja nī ūndū wa kūhihwo tūmaaī mūno nī tūtambi

English: The aphids’ feeding on the plant causes wrinkling and cupping of leaves, defoliation, and stunted growth.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi tūtū tūrīaga mūmera ūyū ūguo mahuti makaambirīria kūgua na mūmera ūkahoha

English: Aphids produce large amounts of a sugary liquid waste called honeydew.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi tūtū nī tūrūtaga tūmaaī twīna cukari mūingī mūno tūrīa twītagwo honey dew

English: A fungus, called sooty mold, grows on honeydew deposits that accumulate on leaves and branches, turning leaves and branches black.
Kikuyu: Fungus ītagwo sooty mold nī īkūraga handū haha na kwa ūguo īgatherema nginya mahutiīnī na īgatūma mathangū hamwe na honge kūira.

Kikuyu: Kūira kwa honge na mahuti nī kīonereria gīa tūtambi

Kikuyu: Ingihota atīa gūthaitīra tūtambi na njīra cia kīmerera?

English: Control and kill ants.
Kikuyu: Kūūraga thuraku

English: Cultivate and flood the field.
Kikuyu: Kūhurania na gwīkīra mūgūnda maaī maiganu

English: This will destroy ant colonies and expose eggs and larvae to predators and sunlight.
Kikuyu: ūū nī gūtumaga matumbī ma tūtambi Kuora

English: Ants use the aphids to gain access to nutrients from the plants.
Kikuyu: Thuraku cihūthagīra tūtambi gūkinyīra irio kabici-inī

English: 2. Avoid using heavy doses of highly soluble nitrogen fertilizers.
Kikuyu: 2. Ndūkahūthīre bataraitha īna nitrogen nyingī

English: Aphids love tender, juicy leaves.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi mūthemba wa ūme twendete mathangū marīa mena maaī maingī

English: Practice split application: during seedling and flowering stage.
Kikuyu: Handa mīmera yaku īraihanīrīirie

Kikuyu: 3. Handanīria mboga na indo ta gītungūru,saumu, spider plant na ndania

English: 5. Plant in a well prepared fertile seedbed to promote crop vigour.
Kikuyu: 5. Handa mbegū mūgūnda-inī mūnoru nīgūo ūkūre ūrī na hinya.

English: 6. Keep the fields free of weeds and alternative hosts such as wild mustards.
Kikuyu: 6. Theria mūgūnda na mīmera yoothe īngīhitha tūtambi

English: 7. Manage the field to favour natural enemies such as lady bird beetles, hover flies and Aphids.
Kikuyu: 7. Tigīrīra atī mūgūnda waku wīna īndo īria ciingataga tūtambi

English: 8. Encourage growth of milkweed within the field. Milkweed is a host of other types of aphids which are natural enemies
Kikuyu: 8. Tigīrīra mūgūnda waku wīna ria mūthemba wa milkweed. Milkweed īkoragwo gīikaro gīa tūtambi mūthemba ūrīa ūrī ūguni nīūndū nī thū ya tūtambi tūrīa tūthūkagia kabici.

English: 9. Mulch with wheat straw or clear/transparent polyethylene sheet as reflectors of UV light discourage aphids from landing on the crop.
Kikuyu: 9. Humbira mīmera na mahuti kana maratathi ma mīnyore marahītūkia ūtheri na marīa magiragia ūme kūūmba mūmera-inī.

English: 10.Use overhead irrigation to knock aphids off the leaves.
Kikuyu: 10. Itirīria mboga maaī ūkīhūthīra jeti nīgetha ūme wūitike.

English: At what time should I monitor for aphids on my cabbages?
Kikuyu: Nī rī njagīrīirwo gūkorwo ngīthaitīra tūtambi

Kikuyu: Warīkia kūruta mīmera yaku kīareini na kūhanda harīa hagīrīire

English: • Look at the lower leaves, stems and growing point for soft bodied insects, curled or capping of infested leaves, black sooty mould.
Kikuyu: Rora mathangu mwena wa nathīī, honge na kūria kūhoro kana kwīna dariri cia tūtambi kūbutha kana mūrimū oo wothe

English: • Institute control measures when 1 to 2 % of plants are infested
Kikuyu: Rūmīrīra njīra iria ciagīrīire wona mūmera ūmwe kana īrī yahutio nī tūtambi

Kikuyu: No ūhūthire maaī ma thabuni kūhuhīra mīmera īhutītio nī ūme na ingīthondeka atīa mūtukanio ūyū?

English: Ammonia spray • Mix 1 part ammonia with 7 parts water.
Kikuyu: Tukania gīcunji kimwe kīa Ammonia na maaī icunjī mugwanja

English: This spray also controls flea beetles.
Kikuyu: Dawa īno ni yūragaga flea beetles

English: • Mix 2½ tablespoons of liquid soap to a gallon of water. 21
Kikuyu: Īkīra iciko igīrī na nuthu cia thabuni maaī mūigana wa lita 21

English: • Spray soapy solution (potassium soaps) (add 10-15 table spoon full of liquid soap in 20lt bucket and spray).
Kikuyu: Huhīra mūmera wakū mūtukanio ūyū

English: Avoid powder detergent soaps because they can burn plant leaves and if used frequently, will reduce soil fertility.
Kikuyu: Ndūkahūthīre thabuni utari wa maaī nīundu no ucine mūmera waku

English: How do I control cabbage head caterpillar in cabbages?
Kikuyu: Ingihota atīa kūūraga kīgūnyū gīa kabicī?

English: The cabbage plant has ‘window-liked’ damage on its outer leaves.
Kikuyu: Mathangū matūrikagio marima maingī.

English: Larval damage during early head formation results in an aborted or multiple heads.
Kikuyu: Matumbī ma kīgunyū kīa mboga matūmaga mboga īgīe na tuongo makīria ma kamwe

English: The larva bores holes on the developing head. A damaged developing head contains waste matters.
Kikuyu: Matumbī macarīkaga igunyū iria irīaga nathī-inī mboga īkīūmba ūndū ūtūmaga īgīe marima. Mboga nyanange īkoragwo na kīoro kīa igunyū.

English: Attract almost the entire population of the cabbage head caterpillar and 90% DBM.
Kikuyu: Kūgucagīrīria igunyū ciothe cia mboga na gīcunje kīa mīrongo kenda kīa DBM.

English: Remove the trap crops when these are heavily infested with the pests or else these pests will transfer to the main crop.
Kikuyu: Ruta mahuti marīa magūmīirwo nī tūtambi kwa ūgūo, tūtambi tūu tūgūtherema nginya kūrī mūmera ūrīa wa bata.

English: Plant Indian mustard (Brassica juncea) as a trap crop between several rows of common cabbage to attract most cabbage head caterpillar moths and some Diamondback moth (DBM) adults.
Kikuyu: Handa mūmera mūthemba wa indian mastard (Brassica junce) gatagatī-inī ka raini cia mboga nīgūo kūhenereria kīhuruta gīa kīgunyū kīa mboga hamwe na DBM.

English: Mustard Larvae feed primarily on leaves causing irregular, rugged holes, bore through the pods, and contaminate pods and leaves with their frass(excreta).
Kikuyu: Kīgunyū kīa mūmera wa masterd kīrīaga mahuti gīgatūma magīe mūhianīre ūngī, marima, gīgatūra mīciri, na gūtiga kīoro mahuti-inī.

English: Plants can be severely defoliated and stunted. Sometimes, they damage the seedlings, but heavy injury usually occurs during pod stage.
Kikuyu: Mīmera nī īkuraga kahora mūno na rīmwe īgathūka oo biū. Rīmwe, ithūkagia mbeū, no kaingī ūthūkangia mūnene wonekaga mbeū igīkīra.

English: Attract almost the entire population of the cabbage head caterpillar and 90% DBM.
Kikuyu: Kūgucagīrīria igunyū ciothe cia mboga na gīcunje kīa mīrongo kenda kīa DBM.

English: Remove the trap crops when these are heavily infested with the pests or else these pests will transfer to the main crop.
Kikuyu: Eheria mīmera yoothe īrīa īhithaga tūtambi nīūndū no tūthamīre mboga-inī.

English: How do I recognise black rot on my cabbages?
Kikuyu: Kīonereria kīa mūrimū wa kūbutha kwa mboga nīkīrī kū?

English: Black-rot, caused by the bacterium Xanthomonascampestrispv.campestris, is the most serious disease of crucifers in Kenya.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ūrehagwo ni bacteria mūthemba wa Xanthomonasccampestrispv.campestris nīguo mūthemba ūrīa mūru mūno ūhutagia mbarī ya crucifers būrūri-inī wa Kenya.

English: The bacterium attacks many species of the Brassica family.
Kikuyu: Bacteria īno ni īgūmagira ona mīmera īngī mūthemba wa mboga

English: Among these are cabbage, collard, kale, mustard and turnip.
Kikuyu: Imwe cia cīo nī kabici, thūkūma,collard,turnip ma mastered

English: Plants may be affected at any stage of growth.
Kikuyu: Igumagīra handū o hothe harī ukūria wa kabici

English: This disease is seed borne and is often introduced by contaminated seeds or infected transplants.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū umanaga na mbegū cīumīte mūmera-inī mūrwaru kana mīūngūrua mīrwaru.

English: In some areas of the country, the disease is of minor importance; however, in some parts of Kenya the conditions, the disease becomes serious and many growers sustain severe economic loss.
Kikuyu: Kūrī icigo imwe būrūri-inĩ, mūrimū ūcio ndūkoragwo na bata mūno; no icigo-inī imwe būrūri-inī wa Kenya, mūrimū ūcio ūkoragwo na ūritū mūno na arĩmi aingī nīmonaga hathara nene.

English: In some cases, the crop may be destroyed. In the field, the disease is easily recognized by the presence of large yellow to yellow orange V-shaped lesions extending inward from the margin of the leaf.
Kikuyu: Ihinda-inī rĩmwe, kīmera kīu no gīthūke. Thĩinī wa mūgūnda, mūrimū ūcio nīūmenyekaga wega nīūndū wa ironda nene cia rangi wa ngoikoni nginya yellow-orange ĩhaana ta V ĩratambūrūrūkĩra na thĩinī kuuma mīthia-inī ya mathangū.

English: When infected seeds germinate, the resulting young plants usually die quickly; however, these plants serve as an inoculum source for other plants.
Kikuyu: Hĩndī ĩrĩa mbeū iria irīna mūrimū ūcio yahandwo, mĩmera ĩrĩa ĩmeraga nayo nīikuaga na ihenya; no mĩmera ĩyo ĩihūthīkaga na kīhumo kīa inoculum kīa mīmera īngī.

English: If infection occurs in young seedlings, the disease is much more severe because the main stem becomes infected and the disease becomes systemic and moves throughout the plant.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūcio ūngīkorwo mīmera-inī mīnini, ūkoragwo ūrī mūūru mūno tondū gītina nī ūnyitagwo nī mūrimū ūcio na ūgathiī na mbere gūtherema thīinī wa mūtī wothe.

English: These plants remain stunted and the veins in the stem are black. Heads developed from these plants deteriorate rapidly after harvest.
Kikuyu: Mĩmera ĩyo nī īhotomaga na mĩri ĩkoragwo ĩrī mīirū. Mboga ithūkaga naihenya thutha wa kūgethwo.

English: The bacterium enters the vascular system of the plant principally through natural openings and injuries on the leaf.
Kikuyu: Bacteria ĩyo ĩtoonyaga thĩinī wa mĩri ya mūtī ūcio kūgerera tūrima-inī twa mūtī na ironda irī mathangū-inĩ.

English: In time, the bacterium spreads in the vascular system of the leaf and stem.
Kikuyu: Thutha-inī ītheremaga mathangū-inī mothe

English: The disease spreads and causes most damage in wet, warm weather.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūcio ūtheremaga mūno rĩrĩa kūrīna wūigū, na ūrugarĩ.

English: It does not usually spread in dry weather and is inactivated at temperatures below 50°F.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ndūonekaga mūno kūndu kwīna riūa na ikīro cia rugarī ciakīra 50° gūikūrūka nī itūmaga mūrimū ūyū wage kamweke

English: The bacterium can survive in the soil for 14 to 42 days, depending on the season, and in old cabbage stems for 244 days.
Kikuyu: Njamuthi ĩyo no ĩtūūre thĩinī wa tĩri matukū 14 nginya 42, kūringana na ihinda rĩa mwaka, na no īikare thīinī wa mūkori wa kabici thikū 244.

English: The disease is also carried over on weed hosts such as “pepper grass” and with wild species of mustard, radish and turnip.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūcio ningī nīūtambagio nī mĩmera ĩngī ta pepper grass na ria mĩthemba ya mustard, radish na turnip.

English: The bacterium is spread by splashing rain, irrigation and running surface water as well as insects and other movement in the field while the plants are wet.
Kikuyu: Bacteria ĩyo ĩtambagio nī matata ma mbura, maaī ma kūrīma na kīguū, o hamwe na igunyū na gūcera mūgūnda-inī mīmera ītaitīte ūmwe.

English: Disease management strategies such as crop rotation away from contaminated land for two years and use of certified seed or transplants are effective.
Kikuyu: Mĩtaratara ya kūgirĩrĩria gūtherema kwa mūrimū ta kwaga mūhanda mūgūnda ūrīa mūrwaru kwa ihinda rīa mīaka īrī na kūhūthĩra mbeū kana mĩūngūrwa ĩrĩa mīhetūkie nīkūrehaga moimĩrĩra mega.

English: There is no remedial control for this disease.
Kikuyu: Gūtirī dawa ya kūhonia mūrimū ūyū.

Kikuyu: Ingĩhota atīa kūgirĩrĩria kūbutha kwa kabici?

Kikuyu: Hūthĩra mĩthemba ĩrĩa ĩrīīhotaga gwītiria mīrimū ta Baraka F1, Globe Master F1, Blue dynasty FI, Riana F1.

English: • Rotate with non-brassicas like maize and legumes for at least 2 years.
Kikuyu: • Handa mīthemba īngī ītarī ya bamīrī ya brassicas ta mbembe na mīthemba ya mboco kwa ihinda rīa mīaka īrī.

Kikuyu: • Nīwega kweheria ria rīothe nīguo rītigakorwo rītukanīte na mīthemba īngī kuuma mbari cia brassica.

English: • Alternate dry and wet spells in the soil
Kikuyu: • Nīwega kūgarūrīra ihinda rīrīa tīri ūkoragwo ūrī mūmū na mūigū.

English: • Crop rotation Monitoring.
Kikuyu: • Kūrūmĩrĩra maūmīrīra ma mūtaratara wa kūgarura mīmera īrīa īrahandwo mūgūnda.

English:
Monitor for
• Yellow, brown v-shaped patches at the edge of the leaves.
Kikuyu: Rora maūndū maya; kana mathangū nīmarethodeka maroro marī na mūhianīre wa V na makagarūrūka rangi magakorwo ma ngoikoni na gītīri mīthia-inī.

English: • If the yellow/black lesion is held against the light, black veins are visible.
Kikuyu: • Tūmīkiha tūīirū/ngoikoni nītwonekaga maroro maya mangīrorerwo ūtheri-inĩ.

English: • A cross section of infected stem reveals a black ring.
Kikuyu: • Mūkori wa kabici mūrwaru ūngīrengwo wonanangia gīthiūrūrī kia rangi mūirū.

English: • In later stages, head turns black with an offensive foul smell.
Kikuyu: • Mūrimū ūngīneneha, mūtwe wa kabici ūgarūrūkaga rangī ūgatwīka mūirū na ūgacoka ūkanunga mūno.

English: • Disease is common during wet conditions with high temperatures
Kikuyu: • Mūrimū ūyū ūingīhaga hīndī ya heho na ūrugarī mūingĩ.

English: • Take immediate action when first symptoms appear.
Kikuyu: • Oya itua rīa naihenya ndariri cia mbere cioneka.

English: How do I recognise black rot on my cabbages?
Kikuyu: Ingīmenya atīa atī kabici nīnyite nī mūrimū wa kūbutha, black rot?

English: Bacillus thuringiensis;Beauveria Bassiana;Amblseiuscalifornicus (Predatory mites)Bacillus thuringiensis is a Biological insecticide for control of Lepidoptera larva and other pests on vegetables eg Giant Lopper, green lopper, leaf skeletonizers and jelly grab in coffee.

Kikuyu: Brassicas: Rĩĩtwa rĩa bamīrī ya  mĩmera ta kabici, rape, broccoli, kales, turnips, na ingī nīūndū wa gūkorwo irī cia bamīrī ya brassicacae.

English: This family is also known as Cruciferae so some people call these crucifers
Kikuyu: Bamĩrī ĩno ningī nīyuīkaine ta Cruciferae na kwoguo andū amwe maciītaga crucifers

Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa kūbutha, kana black rot, ūtambaga atīa?

English: It may survive in disease crop residues buried in soil up to 2 years.
Kikuyu: No ūtūūre mĩaka ūrī thĩinī wa matigari ma mĩmera mūthike tīri-inī.

English: Green challenger, Riana and Pruktor
Kikuyu: Green challenger, Riana na Pruktor

English: How do I control black rot in cabbages?
Kikuyu: Ingĩhota atĩa kūgirĩrĩria kabici kūbutha?

Kikuyu: Arĩa marutaga wĩra mĩgūnda-inī īrīa īna mūrimū, magĩrĩirwo gwĩthamba moko na kūhura indo ciao cia wĩra makĩhūthĩra Jik.

English: These include • green challenger,• Riana and Pruktor
Kikuyu: Mīthemba īno nī hamwe na; green challenger, Riana na Pruktor

Kikuyu: Kūhĩa mīthia kūrĩa kuonekaga thĩinī wa kabici: kūhĩa kūu kūrehagwo nī wagi wa calcium ya kūigana.

English: maintenance of a uniform soil moisture content is done to prevent moisture stresses.
Kikuyu: Ūigananĩru wa ūīgū tīri-inī nīwagīrīire nĩguo mīmera ndīgatangīke nīūndū wa wagi wa maaī.

English: Tip burn causes the tip to become necrotic.
Kikuyu: Kūhīa mīthiani nī gutūmaga mīthia icenjie rangi

English: Spraying of the calcium does not necessarily relieve the symptoms.
Kikuyu: Kūhūra mītī calcium no kwage kūigana kūnina thīna ūyū.

English: Calcium normally requires root pressure to move into the leaves, especially the inner leaves.
Kikuyu: Nĩguo calcium ĩingĩre mathangū-inĩ, makĩria mathangū-inī marĩa marīthĩinĩ, no mūhaka mĩri ĩkorūo na hinya.

English: What are the factors to consider in selecting site for cabbage nursery?
Kikuyu: Nī maūdū marīku mabatiī kūrorwo rīrīa ūrathodeka gīito kana tuta ya kabici?

English: Avoid setting up the nursery in fields previously having a Cabbage crop
Kikuyu: Ndūgathodeke tuta hadū herīga kūhadwo kabici.

English: What is the appropriate time for transplanting of cabbage seedlings after planting?
Kikuyu: Nī hīndī īrīkū njega ya gūthamia mīūngūrūa kuuma giito-inī?

English: Seedlings are transplanted 30 days after seed germination
Kikuyu: Mīūngūrūa īhadagwo thutha wa thikū mīrongo ītatū kuuma ya mera tuta-ini.

English: .It is recommended that transplanting should be done either early in the morning or late in the evening
Kikuyu: Nīkwedekithītio kūhanda mīūngūrūa mathaa-inī ma rūcini kīroko kana hwa-inī gūgituka.

English: What is the recommended spacing for cabbage production.
Kikuyu: Kabici ibatiī gūtigithanio atīa rīrīa ikūhadwo?

English: The recommended spacing is 60 cm between rows and 45 – 60 cm between plants depending on the variety
Kikuyu: Nikwedekithītio ūtaganu wa sentimita 60 kuuma mūhari ūmwe nginya ūrīa ūngī na sentimita 45-60 kuuma mūmera ūmwe nginya ūrīa ūngī kūringana na mūhīrīga wa kabici.

English: What are some of the post harvest handling practices for cabbages?
Kikuyu: Nī mamwe marīkū ma maūdū mabatiī kūrūmīrīrwo thutha wa kūgetha kabici?

English: Packed in clean well ventilated containers/crates and transported in covered vehicles
Kikuyu: Kūbangūo wega thīinī wa mathadūkū matheru na mena rīera rīiganu na ikuo na ngari ngunīke.

English: Where can I get propagation materials for cabbage production?
Kikuyu: Ingīruta kū mbegū cia kabici cia kūhada?

English: Certified cabbage seeds are acquired from registered seed companies or stockists, then the farmer raises seedlings or seedlings can be acquired from registered commercial nurseries.
Kikuyu: Mbegū thūrime cia kabici ciumaga kūrī kambuni iria nyendekithie cia mbegū kana nduka-inī cia ido cia ūrīmī na thutha ucio murīmi agakūria mīūngurūa kana mīūngūrwa no yoneke kuuma kūrī kambuni cia gūitithia.

English: What are the various methods of seedling preparation in cabbage production?
Kikuyu: Njīra imwe cia kūharīria mīūngurūa ya kabici nī irīkū?

English: Seedlings should be grown in a well-aerated medium, which has good water holding capacity and at a pH of around 6.5.
Kikuyu: Mīūngurūa ībatiī gūkūrio handū hega hena rīera na maaī maigananīru na tīri ūrī na ūrūrū wa 6.5 pH.

English: Generally, peat, bark and vermiculite mixes are used.
Kikuyu: Kaingī mīūngūrwa īkūrio mūtukanio-inī wa peat, bark na vermiculite.

English: Media problems typically include excessive tannins and low air filled porosity, which results in poor drainage and the build up of green mould.
Kikuyu: Mathīna mamwe ma mītukanio īno ya gūkūrīria nī ta ūingī wa tannins na kwaga rīera rīiganu īgacungīrīria kwaga kūhetūkia maaī wega na kūmera kwa buu cia ngirini.

English: The medium should be pre-enriched and the seedlings should be fertilized.
Kikuyu: Mītukanio ya gūkūrīrio ibatiī kūongererwo ūnoru wa kūigana na mīūngūrwa īkaheo bataraitha.

English: For optimum germination, the seedling trays should be placed in a germination chamber, at 20 °C with high relative humidity.
Kikuyu: Nīguo mbeū ciothe imere wega, turee cīa kūhandīra mbeū ibatiī kūigwo nyūmba-inī cia kūmerithia iria irī na ūrugarī wa 20°C na ūgunyu ūtarī mūingī.

English: The seedlings should be moved to the tunnel at the first sign of germination.
Kikuyu: Mīūngūrwa ibatiī gūtwarwo muungu-inī cironeka ikīmera.

English: The ideal temperature for seedling cultivation is 20 °C
Kikuyu: Ūrugarī ūrīa mwega mūno wa kūmerithia mīūngūrūa nī 20°C.

English: What are some of the things to avoid in seedling  management at the nursery?
Kikuyu: Nī maūdū marīkū ūbatiī gwīthema ūkīramata gīito?

English: • Incorrect sowing time. Cold temperatures, particularly below 7 °C.Cold grown seedlings.
Kikuyu: Kūhura mbeū ihinda rīrīa rītabatiī. Heho nene īrathiī thīī wa 7°C. Gūkūria mīūngūrwa hīndī ya heho.

English: Over-fertilization of seedlings.
Kikuyu: Gūīkīra bataraitha nyingī gūkīra gīthimi mīūngūrwa-inī.

English: Oversized seedlings at transplant.
Kikuyu: Ūraihu mūkīru wa mīūngūrwa hīndī ya gūthamia.

English: Temperature differences between the seedling nursery and the farm.
Kikuyu: Ngūrani ya ūrugarī gīito-inī na mūgūnda.

English: Outbreak of the pest and diseases
Kikuyu: Mītuthūko ya tūtabi na mīrimū.

English: How is sowing of seeds done in the seed bed?
Kikuyu: Mbeū ihuragwo atīa gīito-inī?

English: In the seedbed, sow 15 -20 seeds per 30 cm (1 foot) at a depth of 0.5–1.0 cm.
Kikuyu: Kara tūmītaro tūrī na ūraihu wa sentimita mīrongo ītatū(futi īmwe) na ūriku wa kuuma sentimita 0.5 nginya 1.0 na ūhure mbeū ūkūmi na ithano nginya mīrongo īrī o kamūkaro.

English: In seed trays (grown under greenhouse), sow 2–3 seeds per hole at a depth of 0.5–1.0 cm
Kikuyu: Thīinī wa turee(ikūrīrio thīinī wa greenhouse), hura mbeū igīrī kana ithatū o irima rīrī na ūriku wa sentimita 0.5 nginya 1.0.

English: Optimum temperature requirement for germination is 20–25°C.
Kikuyu: Nīguo mīūngūrūa īmere, ibataraga mūigana wa ūrūgarī wa 20–25°C.

English: The seeds germinate after 3–5 days
Kikuyu: Mbeū cimeraga thutha wa thikū ithatū nginya ithano.

English: How is thinning done?
Kikuyu: Hihi mīūngūrwa īthathaūragwo atīa?

English: In seed trays, leave only 1 healthy seedling (removing 1-2 unhealthy ones) during the first leaf stage
Kikuyu: Tigia mbeū imwe irīa īna riri o irima thīinī wa turee thutha wa kūruta mathangū ma mbere.

English: When is hardening done and how is it done?
Kikuyu: Kūūmīrīria gwikagwo atīa na rī?

English: Five (5) days before transplanting, gradually expose seedlings daily to strong sunlight and also reduce the water supply to lessen stress of the seedlings after transplanting
Kikuyu: Thikū ithano mbere ya gūthamia mbeū kuuma natharī-inī, ciotithie rīua ndagīka nini o mūthenya nīguo cigīe na ūkindīrīku na kūūmīrīria, ningī ūcoke ūcinyihīrie maaī nīguo imenyere na itigatangīke ciathamio kuuma natharī-inī.

English: How is cabbage transplanting done?
Kikuyu: Mīūngūrūa ya kabici īthamagio atīa kuuma natharī-inī?

English: Seedlings ready for transplanting after 4-6 weeks in the nursery, depending on temperatures.
Kikuyu: Mīūngūrwa ibatiī gūthamio kuuma natharī-inī thutha wa wiki inya nginya ithathatū, na ningī kūringana na ūrugarī.

English: Wet the seedlings an hour before transplanting.
Kikuyu: Gunyia natharī ithaa rīmwe mbere ya gūkūra mīūngūrwa.

English: Plant to the same depth as in the nursery 15cm.
Kikuyu: Hada na ūriku o ta wa natharī wa sentimita ikūmi na ithano.

English: Use Optimizer 10ml/20L to relieve transplanting shock.
Kikuyu: Hūra optimizer mūigana wa mililita ikūmi ītukanītio na maaī rita mīrongo īrī nīguo kūgiria mīūngūrwa gūtangīka thutha wa gūthamio.

English: How is cabbage transplanting done?
Kikuyu: Mīūngūrwa ya kabici īthamagio atīa kuuma natharī-inī?

English: The grower should ensure that seedlings are planted at the correct depth in a little hole that has been formed into the ground prior to planting.
Kikuyu: Mūrīmi nī abatiī gūtigīrīra mīūngūrwa nī yahandwo na ūriku wa marima ūrīa wagīrīire marīa mabatiī kwenjwo thikū ciganona mbere ya mahanda.

English: If the seedlings are forced into the ground, without a hole being prepared for them to be inserted into, the root system will be compromised and the plant will experience stress resulting in poor yield.
Kikuyu: Mīūngūrwa īhinyīrīrio tīiri-inī tondū handu hatarī na irima rīenje wega, mīri ndīkūraga wega na nīūndū ūcio mūmera ūkahinyīrīrīka na magetha makanyiha.

English: Once the seedling is placed inside the hole the area should be firmed so that sufficient contact is made between the seedling and the soil.
Kikuyu: Thutha wa mīūngūrūa kūhandwo marima-inī nī harī bata wa gūkindīra wega nīguo mīrī īhutanie wega na tīri.

English: When planting seedlings a choice can be made between the square method and the staggered method of planting.
Kikuyu: Ukihada mīūngūrwa rī, nī ubatīe ūthūre njīra ya kuhada, no uhande na njira ya mathadūkū kana kūnyagia.

English: The staggered method is more advantageous as there is less competition between plants compared to the square method where plants are directly opposite each other, thus maximizing competition
Kikuyu: kūhanda ūnyagītie kwī kīguni tondū gūtirehaga macindano ūringithanītie na gūthimithania marima kūria mīmera īringanīte mīena yothe.

English: What are some of the value addition techniques in cabbage production?
Kikuyu: Ni njira irikū hihi cia mwanya cia ūkūria wa kabicī.

English: Sorting: Damaged and diseased heads are discarded
Kikuyu: Gūthuura. Mītwe irīa mīthūku na mīrwarū ibatīe nī gūteo.

English: • Grading: Cabbages are graded depending on the head size: small (1 – 2 kg), medium (3 – 4 kg), large (over 5 kg)
Kikuyu: Kabici ithuragwo kūringana na thaithi.

English: What are the main types of cabbage grown in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nini nī kilo (1kg-2kg) gatagatīīnī kilo (3kg-4kg) nene ni kilo 5kg gūthiī na igūrū

English: Three main types: Green, Red and Savoy
Kikuyu: mitheba itatū mīnene ni Green, Red na Savoy

English: What are some of the common varieties of cabbage grown in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī īriku imwe ya mītheba ya Kabici īrīa īkūragio būrūrinī wa Kenya

English: Gloria F1”Copenhagen Market”Blue Dynasty F1”Baraka F1,“Green Challenger F1”“Pruktor F1”“Riana F1,“Amigo F1”
Kikuyu: Gloria F1”Copenhagen Market”Blue Dynasty F1”Baraka F1,“Green Challenger F1”“Pruktor F1”“Riana F1,“Amigo F1”

English: What are the characteristics of F1?
Kikuyu: Maūthī ma mbūga mūthemba wa F1 nī marīkū?

English: A medium-late maturing variety ready for harvesting 90 days after transplanting.
Kikuyu: Nī ta gatagatīīni na gūcererwo kūgimara na īngīgethwo thutha wa thikū 90

English: Has solid blue green color and thick waxy layer.
Kikuyu: Ikoragwo na rangi wa blue na ngirini na kīmaaī

English: It is not prone to splitting and keeps well after harvesting.
Kikuyu: Ndiatūkaga na nī īikaraga thutha wa kūgethwo

English: Tolerant to Black Rot and resistant to Fusarium yellows.Maturity: 90 days after transplanting (medium-late maturing).
Kikuyu: Niumagirīrīa black rot na nīītiragia Fusarium yellows. Ikūraga thutha wa thikū 90 kuma kūhada.

English: Plant Spacing: 60 cm x 60 cm.
Kikuyu: Gūtigithūkanio wa mīūngūrwa nī sentimita 60 x60

English: Average head weight 3-5 kg.
Kikuyu: irī  wa kilo 3-5 hari kabici ciothe

English: Yield: 30,000-50,000kg per īka
Kikuyu: Magetha nī kilo 30,000-50,000 o harī īka ya mūgūnda

English: What are the characteristics of coppen haggen market variety?
Kikuyu: Nī mītugo īrīkū īhithūragīa harī mīūngūrwa ya Kabici mūtheba wa coppen haggen.

English: Requires a cool/warm climate.
Kikuyu: Nīyedaga rīera rīa mūiganīra, urūgarī na ūgūnyū

English: Plant Spacing: 60 cm x 45 cm.
Kikuyu: Mīūngūrwa gūtigithūkanio sentimita 60x45.

English: Plant population: 14,800 per īka.
Kikuyu: mīmera yothe harī o īka ni 14,800

English: Maturity: 65 – 70 days after transplanting (early maturing).
Kikuyu: Gūkūra nī thutha wa thīkū 65-70 thutha wa kūruta mīūngūrwa kuuma natharīīni.( gūkū ni gūkūra kwa naihenya)

English: Head: small to medium round shape with uniform size, weighing 2 – 2.5 kg.
Kikuyu: Nini nginya cia thaīthi cīgananīrie cia ūbwo wa mūthirīngo na ciothe nī cigananīte

English: Yield: 20,000 – 30,000kg per īka
Kikuyu: Magetha nī kilo 30,000-50,000 o harī īka ya mūgūda

English: What are the characteristics of blue dynasty F1?
Kikuyu: Nī mītugo irīku ya mwanya ya mīūngūrwa ya blue dynasty F1?

English: Good tolerance to Black Rot, Cabbage Ringspot, Diamondback Moth (DBM) & Fusarium Yellows.
Kikuyu: Nīītiragia mīrimū ta Black Rot, Cabbage Ringspot, Diamondback Moth (DBM) & Fusarium Yellows.

English: Can do well in warm areas.
Kikuyu: No igacīre wega kundū kwina rugarī

English: What are the factors to consider when choosing a cabbage variety to plant?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū mabatīe kūrorwo na kinyi ūgīthura mūtheba wa kabici wa kūhanda

English: What are the characteristics of baraka F1?
Kikuyu: Ni mītugo irīku ya mūtheba wa kabici wa Baraka F1

English: What are the characteristics of  green challenger 1?
Kikuyu: Ni mītugo irīku ya mūtheba wa kabici wa green challenger F1

English: What are the characteristics of Pruktor F1
Kikuyu: Ni mītugo irīku ya mūtheba wa kabici wa pruktor F1

English: What are the characteristics of Riana F1?
Kikuyu: Ni mītugo irīku ya mūtheba wa kabici wa Riana F1

English: What are the characteristics of Amigo  F1?
Kikuyu: Ni mītugo irīku ya mūtheba wa kabici wa Amigo F1

English: While making a choice on the variety to produce, a farmer need to consider several key factors among them the maturity duration,
Kikuyu: Nigūo gūthuura mūtheba wa kabici wa kūhanda, mūrīmī ni abatiī kūrora maūndū matīganīte, ari mo ni ihinda rīa mūthemba ūcio gūkūra.

English: yield potential, tolerance and resistance to pests and diseases, good field holding capacity, uniform maturity to ensure a single harvest and preference in the market among other qualities.
Kikuyu: ūhotekeku wa mūtheba ūcīo wa kabici gūciara,wikindirīkū na gwītiria tūtambi na mīrimū, mūgūnda kuīnyītia, gūkūra hamwe nīguo magetha magakorwo ni maratwarana na wendekithia wa thoko arī imwe ya ikīro irīa ibatiī.

English: What are cabbage water requirements during production?
Kikuyu: Ūkūria wa Kabici ūrabatara  maaī mūigana  ūrīkū?

English: Cabbage needs sufficient amount, although young cabbage transplants or seedlings have a lower water requirement.
Kikuyu: Kabici ibataraga maaī ma mūiganira. No rīrī, mīūngūrwa ībataraga maaī o manīnī.

English: However, water requirement increases rapidly as complete canopy cover and head development occurs.
Kikuyu: No harī oūguo, ūingī wa maaī harī mūmera ni ūbataranagīa harī mūmera mathangū maaīngīha na mutwe kūūmīrīria.

English: Water daily until the plants establish fully. Always water the plants when wilting happens.
Kikuyu: He kabici maaī o mūthenya nginyagīa mūmera ūnyite wega kana ungīonia ndariri cia kūhoha.

English: However, the plants are very sensitive to flooding.
Kikuyu: Harī o ugūo rī, kabicī nīithūkagio nī mwaraho wa maaī.

English: During rainy season, appropriate drainage canals must be in placed to drain off the excess water after a heavy rain.
Kikuyu: Mahinda ma mbura nyingī, weji wa mitaro nīuteithagīriria kūrekereria maaī marīa mangīthukia mīmera

English: Irrigation must be closely monitored to ensure soil moisture remains even in the beds during the cropping period.
Kikuyu: uitīīrīria wa maaī ūrīminī nī ūbatiī kūrorwo wega nīguo kūgitīra ūgunyu wa tīri kīmera gīothe.

English: It is particularly important in the days following transplanting when seedlings are establishing new root systems.
Kikuyu: Ni wega kūbacīrīra mīri ya mīūngūrwa rīrīa īrarutwo natharīīni

English: Excessive irrigation promotes root rot and results in leaching of nutrients away from the root zone.
Kikuyu: Maaī mangīkīra mūgūnda-inī nīmatūmaga mīri ībuthe na irio cia mīmera igathiī n maaī.

English: Supplying water in equal amounts throughout the season prevents cracking of the heads.
Kikuyu: Uheī wa maaī maigananīirie kīmera gīothe ni ūbatiī nīguo kūgitīra mītwe ya Kabici gwatūka

English: Application of soluble fertilizers as fertigation can be carried out during a normal irrigation
Kikuyu: kūringia bataraitha nī kwega rīrīa ūraitīīrīria maaī

English: Total water requirement is approximately 440mm.
Kikuyu: Maaī marīa mabatiī nī milimita 440.

English: In wet seasons, as a general guideline apply 10 to 15 mm per week for the first third to half of the growing season, and about 25 mm per week thereafter.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa kwīna ūgunyu, he mīmera kuuma milimita 20-25 harī o wiki nginyagia makirīa ma nuthu kīmera, na thutha rī ūhe milimita 25 harī o wiki

English: In hot days, apply 20 to 25 mm per week for the first third to half of the growing season and 40 to 50 mm per week thereafter
Kikuyu: Rīrīa kwīna ūrugarī mūingī, he mīmera milimita 20-25 ya maaī harī o wiki ngīnyagia nuthu ya kīmera na thutha ūcio ūhe milimita 40-50 cia maaī

English: How to irrigate cabbage
Kikuyu: Kabici ciagīrīirwo nī kūheo maaī atīa?

English: Cabbages need regular irrigation to ensure rapid growth and evenness of maturity.
Kikuyu: kabici nīcibataraga kūheo maaī ihinda kwa ihinda nīguo ikūre naihenya  ohamwe na gūkūra kūigananīirie.

English: They can be irrigated by moveable spray lines, traveling irrigators or solid set, or, if the soil is suitable and water available, flood irrigation.
Kikuyu: Mboga ici no citīīrīrio maaī na njĩra ya kūhūthĩra mīberethi īngīthongithio, njeti cia gūthamio kana ona itarī cia gūthamio kana kūhūthĩra njĩra ya kwarahia maaī angĩkorwo tĩri nīmwega na maaī matirī thīna kuoneka.

English: Cabbages grown in beds will require more irrigation than those grown on the flat.
Kikuyu: Kabicī iria cia gūkūrio bedī īnī niībataraga maaī maaīngī gukīra cia kundū kwaraganu.

English: Soil type and weather will also influence the frequency of irrigation.
Kikuyu: Mūthemba wa tīīrī na rīera nī icenjagia mūtugo wa kūhe Kabici maaī

English: The use of tensiometers or other measuring equipment will improve yields and reduce water costs.
Kikuyu: Uhuthīrī wa macini cīa guthima nī itethagia kuongerera magetha o hamwe na kunyīhia thogora wa maaī ma ūkūria.
